name: cross-margin-view
description: Self-contained walkthrough of cross-asset margin. The scenario builds the borrower's + lender's state from scratch: register + verify both parties, init risk params, create both Token-2022 mints, init both vaults, fund both sides (with explicit signer switching), open a cross-mint loan, then read the unified margin view. Each step that requires a signer routes through the `--keypair` global flag so it is unambiguous who signs what. Useful for asking: how does the pool change as I tighten the SOVEREIGN_BOND haircut? Where does cross-mint netting change the verdict vs siloed lending health?
headline: Build one borrower + one lender + a cross-mint loan from scratch, then watch free margin recompute in one read-only call.

steps.explanation: Initialize the on-chain compliance registry (idempotent for a fresh validator). The signer becomes the initial super-admin.
Register the borrower in the compliance registry. Status starts at PENDING.
Verify the borrower so they can deposit, borrow, and settle.
Initialize the RiskParams PDA. Sets the governance-mutable haircut table the margin view reads on every call.
(Off-CLI) Create the collateral mint (sovereign-bond class) without TransferHook. Capture the printed pubkey as MINT_SOVEREIGN.
(Off-CLI) Create the cash mint (stablecoin class) without TransferHook. Capture the printed pubkey as MINT_USDC.
(Off-CLI) Mint 1_000_000 units of collateral to the borrower's wallet.
(Off-CLI) Mint 1_000_000 units of cash to the lender's wallet.
Initialize the collateral vault for the sovereign-class mint.
Initialize the cash vault for the loan's settlement currency.

Borrower deposits 500_000 units of collateral. Signed by the BORROWER keypair — note the explicit `--keypair` so the depositor is unambiguous (vault deposit uses ssr-cli's global keypair as the depositor identity).
Lender deposits 400_000 units of cash inventory. Signed by the LENDER keypair — again, the `--keypair` flag makes the signer explicit.

Open the cross-mint loan: borrower posts 300_000 sovereign collateral, borrows 200_000 USDC against it. `lending open` takes both keypairs as positional inputs, so signer identity is already explicit here.

Read the unified margin view across both collateral mints — this is the punchline. Haircut-adjusted collateral pool, projected liability (principal + accrued simple interest), and net free margin per cash mint, all in one read-only call.
(Parameter dial) Tighten the SOVEREIGN_BOND haircut from default 5% to 15%. Super-admin signs (same wallet that ran `compliance init-registry`).

Re-read margin. Same accounts, same loan; the haircut change alone moves the free-margin number — that's the prime broker thesis (governance-mutable risk parameters that propagate immediately) in action.
